title: linum corymbiferum subsp. lambesanum
intro: Linum corymbiferum subsp. lambesanum, also known as the Lambesan flax, is a subspecies of the Linum corymbiferum plant, a flowering plant in the family Linaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds which are rich in omega-3 fatty acids and contain anti-inflammatory compounds. The seeds of Linum corymbiferum subsp. lambesanum have been traditionally used to support heart health, reduce inflammation, and improve digestive issues, offering various health benefits. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as oil, capsules, and tea, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of menopause, arthritis, and other conditions.
